legal_note: This is a technical compliance baseline, not legal advice. Confirm with Irish counsel/tax advisor before production launch.

p2b_regulation.scope: EU Regulation 2019/1150 applies when business users (Verified Pros) offer services to consumers through the marketplace.
required_tc_clauses: Clear eligibility, onboarding and suspension/termination grounds for Pros.
Ranking transparency: explain main parameters affecting job feed visibility (distance to Eircode, response rate, review score, verification status, recency).
Differentiated treatment disclosure if platform services/products receive preferential ranking.
Data access terms: what data Pros can access (lead data, messages, analytics), portability/export limits, retention windows.
Complaint handling process for business users with response SLAs.
Mediation clause naming at least two mediators for unresolved platform-business disputes.
Notice periods for T&C updates (minimum 15 days unless legal/security urgency).
Statement on restrictions for multi-homing/parity obligations if any.
operational_controls: Versioned T&C with acceptance timestamps in database.
Automated suspension reason logging and appeal workflow.
Public transparency page for ranking factors and policy updates.

revenue_ie_vat_ecommerce.platform_model: Service marketplace may be principal or agent depending on contractual flow; VAT treatment differs.
requirements: Collect supplier tax residency and VAT number (if registered).
Determine B2C vs B2B supply type and place-of-supply rules for services.
Store invoice records and transaction metadata for Revenue audit readiness.
If platform is deemed supplier for any digital/e-services, assess OSS/IOSS obligations.
Apply Irish VAT invoicing fields: supplier details, VAT rate, VAT amount, invoice date/number.
Keep records for statutory retention period and support CSV/JSON exports.

eircode_api_integration.input_standard: Use 7-character Eircode format with optional space (e.g., D02 X285).

providers.name: Ideal Postcodes
providers.use_case: Autocomplete + premise lookup with Eircode support

implementation_requirements: Debounced lookup endpoint in Next.js API route.
Normalize and uppercase Eircode before storage.
Store both freeform address and structured fields (line1, locality, county, eircode, geolocation if available).
Block form submit when Eircode fails regex or provider returns no validated match.
Cache recent lookups and rate-limit endpoint calls.

kyc_requirements: Collect legal name, DOB, residential address, phone/email for individual sole traders.
Collect company number, directors/beneficial owners for incorporated entities when required.
Capture government ID and, where requested, proof of address.
Monitor Stripe 'requirements.currently_due' and 'eventually_due' fields and block payouts/bidding when overdue.
Use webhook events (account.updated, capability.updated) to sync verification state.

marketplace_payment_pattern.secure_hold: Use PaymentIntent with capture_method=manual to authorize funds when customer accepts a quote.
marketplace_payment_pattern.capture_trigger: Capture only when customer marks job complete or completion timeout policy is met.
marketplace_payment_pattern.commission: Set application_fee_amount to 15% of total; transfer remaining net amount to connected account.
marketplace_payment_pattern.refunds_disputes: Define dispute liability and refund waterfall in T&C and automate via webhook handling.

consumer_clauses: Service request lifecycle, quote validity, cancellation window.
Payment authorization and delayed capture consent.
pro_clauses: Verification prerequisites: insurance, ID, Safe Pass where relevant.
No bidding unless verified status is active.
Commission, VAT, payout timing and Stripe account obligations.
Grounds for suspension, delisting, and appeal under P2B complaint flow.
